schema.description:
Fetch the payment txn based on the order no or session id
also, if the payment txn is not in the accepted state for the
input operation, raise an error

order_no.description: This is an optional identifier used to specify the payment transaction upon which the operation should be performed. You can use either the `order_no` or `session_id` field; at least one of these two identifiers must be provided to select the payment transaction that should be actioned.

session_id.description: Similar to `order_no`, `session_id` is an optional identifier used to specify the payment transaction for the operation. You must provide either `order_no` or `session_id` in order to select the appropriate payment transaction.

operation.description:
* `delete` - delete
* `cancel` - cancel
* `expire` - expire
* `refund` - refund
* `capture` - capture
* `void` - void

amount.description: This field is optional and used to specify the amount you want to action for `refund` and `capture` operations. If not specified, Ottu will attempt to perform the operation on the full amount, or the remaining amount if previous operations have been performed. Keep in mind that `void` operations always concern the full amount; the `amount` field is not considered in this case. The `amount` field can be sent for all operations, but it will only be taken into account for `refund` and `capture` operations.

extra.description: This field is optional and used to specify additional parameters for the operation. **IFG**: Full Refund Details For full refund you don't need to use extra key. Partial Refund Details For operations involving partial refunds, the extra field allows specifying individual ticket orders along with their corresponding refund amounts. This capability ensures precise control over the refund amounts for each ticket within an order. Key Points `passengerDocumentNumber`: It is imperative to include the document number for each ticket involved in the refund operation. This ensures that the refund is accurately applied to the correct tickets within an order. `amount`: Alongside each ticket document number, specify the desired refund amount for that ticket and make sure the type of amount is `INT`. `Excluding Tickets`: If certain tickets within an order should not be refunded, you can specify a refund amount of zero for those tickets. This approach allows for selective refunding within a single order. `Important Note` All tickets intended for refund must be explicitly listed in the refund order details, with their respective refund amounts clearly defined. This requirement ensures comprehensive and error-free processing of partial refunds. Example check in Request samples there is an example showcasing how to structure the extra field for a partial refund request, including specifying refund amounts for tickets
